literature_expectation.expected_transport_trend: Weak disorder should preserve coherent transport, while stronger disorder can be partially mitigated by moderate phase scrambling.
literature_expectation.expected_role_of_disorder: Increasing local irregularity should reduce spreading and lower arrival at the target.
literature_expectation.expected_role_of_phase_scrambling: Moderate phase scrambling may help once coherent motion begins to get trapped by disorder.
literature_expectation.expected_failure_mode: Too much phase scrambling should suppress spreading and reduce transport.

literature_expectation.expected_transport_trend: Symmetry can help or hinder transport depending on target geometry, so ring transport may benefit from moderate phase scrambling.
literature_expectation.expected_role_of_disorder: Disorder should break perfect symmetry and can either help or hinder depending on the baseline interference pattern.
literature_expectation.expected_role_of_phase_scrambling: A moderate amount can break destructive interference and improve arrival at the target.
literature_expectation.expected_failure_mode: Very strong phase scrambling should wash out coherent structure without preserving efficient long-range motion.

literature_expectation.expected_transport_trend: A 2D medium should offer more alternate paths, so transport can remain viable even when one path is degraded.
literature_expectation.expected_role_of_disorder: Stronger irregularity should reduce clean spreading and may create trapped regions.
literature_expectation.expected_role_of_phase_scrambling: A moderate amount may stabilize target arrival by reducing coherent path competition.
literature_expectation.expected_failure_mode: Excessive phase scrambling should turn the motion into slow, heavily damped spreading.
